- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""F2 disambiguation route (I-f2-003): cluster + label candidate snippets."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import os
6	from datetime import datetime, timezone
7	from typing import Any
8	
9	import httpx
10	import numpy as np
11	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
12	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
13	
14	from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.real_completion import OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT, _extract_text
15	from polaris_graph.intake.cluster_labeler import ClusterLabelClient, label_clusters
16	from polaris_graph.intake.disambiguation_clusterer import cluster_candidates
17	
18	router = APIRouter(tags=["disambiguation"])
19	
20	
21	class CandidateSnippet(BaseModel):
22	    text: str = Field(min_length=1)
23	    embedding: list[float] = Field(min_length=1)
24	
25	
26	class DisambiguationRequest(BaseModel):
27	    candidates: list[CandidateSnippet] = Field(min_length=1)
28	    min_cluster_size: int = Field(default=2, ge=2)
29	    max_snippets_per_cluster: int = Field(default=3, ge=1)
30	
31	
32	class ClusterPayload(BaseModel):
33	    cluster_id: int
34	    label: str
35	    sample_snippets: list[str]
36	
37	
38	class DisambiguationResponse(BaseModel):
39	    is_ambiguous: bool
40	    num_clusters: int
41	    clusters: list[ClusterPayload]
42	    server_time_utc: str
43	
44	
45	class _OpenRouterLabelClient:
46	    def __init__(self, api_key: str, model: str) -> None:
47	        self.api_key, self.model = api_key, model
48	
49	    def complete(self, prompt: str, *, max_tokens: int | None = None) -> str:
50	        # I-arch-003 (#1253): self.model is reasoning-first (deepseek); the old 50-token default truncated
51	        # mid-reasoning -> empty content -> RuntimeError. Un-starve to the reasoning-first floor + reasoning ON
52	        # at max effort (env-overridable). The label output is short; max_tokens is a cap billed by usage.
53	        _floor = int(os.environ.get("PG_DISAMBIG_LABEL_MAX_TOKENS", "16384") or "16384")
54	        if max_tokens is None or max_tokens < _floor:
55	            max_tokens = _floor
56	        body = {"model": self.model, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
57	                "temperature": 0.0, "max_tokens": max_tokens,
58	                "reasoning": {"effort": os.environ.get("PG_DISAMBIG_REASONING_EFFORT", "high") or "high"}}
59	        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}", "Content-Type": "application/json",
60	                   "HTTP-Referer": "https://polaris-canada.local",
61	                   "X-Title": "POLARIS F2 Disambiguation"}
62	        with httpx.Client() as client:
63	            r = client.post(OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT, json=body, headers=headers, timeout=30.0)
64	            r.raise_for_status()
65	        text = _extract_text(r.json())
66	        if not text.strip():
67	            raise RuntimeError("OpenRouter returned empty disambiguation label")
68	        return text
69	
70	
71	def _make_openrouter_label_client() -> ClusterLabelClient | None:
72	    key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
73	    if not key:
74	        return None
75	    model = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL", "").strip() or "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"
76	    return _OpenRouterLabelClient(api_key=key, model=model)
77	
78	
79	def get_label_client() -> ClusterLabelClient | None: return _make_openrouter_label_client()
80	
81	
82	@router.post("/disambiguation", response_model=None)
83	def post_disambiguation(req: DisambiguationRequest,
84	        client: ClusterLabelClient | None = Depends(get_label_client)) -> DisambiguationResponse:
85	    dim = len(req.candidates[0].embedding)
86	    if any(len(c.embedding) != dim for c in req.candidates):
87	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail={"error": True,
88	            "code": "embedding_dim_mismatch",
89	            "message": f"All candidate embeddings must have dim={dim}."})
90	    embeddings = np.array([c.embedding for c in req.candidates], dtype=np.float64)
91	    cr = cluster_candidates(embeddings, min_cluster_size=req.min_cluster_size)
92	    now_z = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
93	    if cr.num_clusters in (0, 1):
94	        return DisambiguationResponse(is_ambiguous=False, num_clusters=cr.num_clusters,
95	            clusters=[], server_time_utc=now_z)
96	    if client is None:
97	        raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail={"error": True,
98	            "code": "label_client_unavailable",
99	            "message": "OPENROUTER_API_KEY is unset; cannot label clusters."})
100	    labeled = label_clusters(cr, [c.text for c in req.candidates], client,
101	        max_snippets_per_cluster=req.max_snippets_per_cluster)
102	    return DisambiguationResponse(is_ambiguous=True, num_clusters=cr.num_clusters,
103	        clusters=[ClusterPayload(cluster_id=lc.cluster_id, label=lc.label,
104	            sample_snippets=lc.sample_snippets) for lc in labeled],
105	        server_time_utc=now_z)
106	
107	
108	@router.get("/disambiguation/health")
109	def get_disambiguation_health() -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    return {"status": "ok", "stages": ["cluster_candidates", "label_clusters"],
111	            "label_client": "openrouter" if get_label_client() is not None else "sentinel"}
112

1	"""FastAPI route for F-snowball claim-graph endpoint.
2	
3	`GET /api/runs/{run_id}/graph` returns a cytoscape-format `GraphPayload`
4	built from the V30 AuditIR for one run. Server returns elements + a
5	canonical content hash; the client computes layout positions in a Web
6	Worker (DECISION.md Option B; positions are not populated server-side).
7	
8	Per I-snowball-002 brief APPROVE'd by Codex iter 5 (2026-05-12).
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	import hashlib
14	import json
15	import re
16	from typing import Literal
17	
18	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
19	
20	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
21	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
22	
23	if TYPE_CHECKING:
24	    from polaris_graph.audit_ir.loader import AuditIR
25	
26	router = APIRouter(tags=["graph"])
27	
28	# Lazy imports for audit_ir.{registry,loader} live inside `get_run_graph`
29	# (Codex diff iter 1 P1): module-level imports trigger src/__init__.py →
30	# .env load → optional real-backend wiring, which makes app startup +
31	# this module's import non-hermetic. Keep graph_route import cheap.
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# Type literals
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	NodeType = Literal["sentence", "source", "section", "frame"]
38	EdgeType = Literal["cites", "contradicts", "section_member"]
39	Tier = Literal["T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7"]
40	FrameStatus = Literal["pass", "partial", "fail"]
41	
42	_VALID_TIERS = {"T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7"}
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Pydantic models
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	class NodeData(BaseModel):
50	    id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=300)
51	    type: NodeType
52	    label: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=500)
53	    tier: Tier | None = None
54	    sentence_text: str | None = None
55	    source_url: str | None = None
56	    section_title: str | None = None
57	    frame_status: FrameStatus | None = None
58	    classes: str | None = None
59	
60	
61	class Position(BaseModel):
62	    x: float
63	    y: float
64	
65	
66	class ClaimNode(BaseModel):
67	    data: NodeData
68	    position: Position | None = None  # Option B: None server-side; client populates
69	
70	
71	class EdgeData(BaseModel):
72	    id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=400)
73	    source: str
74	    target: str
75	    edge_type: EdgeType
76	
77	
78	class ClaimEdge(BaseModel):
79	    data: EdgeData
80	
81	
82	class GraphElements(BaseModel):
83	    nodes: list[ClaimNode]
84	    edges: list[ClaimEdge]
85	
86	
87	class GraphDiagnostics(BaseModel):
88	    bibliography_count: int
89	    fallback_source_count: int  # unique evidence_ids referenced but absent from bibliography
90	    missing_reference_occurrence_count: int  # total references (with duplicates)
91	    referenced_unknown_evidence_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
92	
93	
94	class GraphPayload(BaseModel):
95	    elements: GraphElements
96	    run_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
97	    elements_hash: str = Field(min_length=64, max_length=64)
98	    diagnostics: GraphDiagnostics
99	    schema_version: Literal["1.0"] = "1.0"
100	
101	
102	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	# Helpers
104	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	def _slug(text: str) -> str:
107	    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", text.lower()).strip("_")[:80] or "section"
108	
109	
110	def _normalize_frame_status(raw: str) -> FrameStatus | None:
111	    if raw == "pass":
112	        return "pass"
113	    if raw == "partial":
114	        return "partial"
115	    if raw.startswith("fail"):
116	        return "fail"
117	    # I-ready-017 FX-07b leg-2 (#1111) — Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2: the
118	    # frame_coverage honesty override emits status="generation_failed" for a
119	    # contract slot whose drafted prose was fully dropped by strict_verify
120	    # (a pipeline fault — zero verified content for that frame). Map it to
121	    # "fail" so the graph inspector renders the frame node as a failed slot
122	    # instead of leaving frame_status null (uncolored). is_pipeline_fault on
123	    # the manifest still carries the finer-grained classification.
124	    if raw == "generation_failed":
125	        return "fail"
126	    # I-ready-017 FX-07b leg-2 (#1111) root-cause design: a validated entity that
127	    # produced zero substantive verified prose (curator must supply licensed
128	    # full-text). Renders as a failed frame in the inspector; human_completion_
129	    # eligible / is_pipeline_fault on the manifest carry the curator-vs-engineer
130	    # routing.
131	    if raw == "curator_gap_no_substantive_content":
132	        return "fail"
133	    return None
134	
135	
136	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137	# Builder (pure function — easy to test)
138	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139	
140	def build_graph_payload(ir: "AuditIR") -> GraphPayload:
141	    """Transform AuditIR into cytoscape-format GraphPayload (deterministic)."""
142	    # 1. Bibliography source nodes
143	    bib_ids: set[str] = set()
144	    nodes: list[ClaimNode] = []
145	    for entry in ir.bibliography:
146	        bib_ids.add(entry.evidence_id)
147	        tier = entry.tier if entry.tier in _VALID_TIERS else None
148	        label = (entry.statement or entry.evidence_id)[:200]
149	        nodes.append(ClaimNode(data=NodeData(
150	            id=f"src:{entry.evidence_id}", type="source", label=label,
151	            tier=tier, source_url=entry.url or None,
152	        )))
153	
154	    # 2. Collect all referenced evidence_ids from sentence tokens + contradictions
155	    referenced_occurrences: list[str] = []
156	    for section in ir.verified_report.sections:
157	        for sent in section.sentences:
158	            if sent.is_verified:
159	                for tok in sent.tokens:
160	                    referenced_occurrences.append(tok.evidence_id)
161	    for cluster in ir.contradictions:
162	        for claim in cluster.claims:
163	            referenced_occurrences.append(claim.evidence_id)
164	    referenced_unique = set(referenced_occurrences)
165	
166	    # 3. Fallback source nodes for referenced-but-missing-from-bibliography
167	    missing_unique = sorted(referenced_unique - bib_ids)
168	    for eid in missing_unique:
169	        nodes.append(ClaimNode(data=NodeData(
170	            id=f"src:{eid}", type="source", label=eid, classes="bibliography_missing",
171	        )))
172	    valid_source_ids = bib_ids | set(missing_unique)
173	
174	    # 4. Section nodes
175	    for section in ir.verified_report.sections:
176	        nodes.append(ClaimNode(data=NodeData(
177	            id=f"section:{_slug(section.title)}", type="section",
178	            label=section.title, section_title=section.title,
179	        )))
180	
181	    # 5. Frame nodes (FrameCoverageReport.entries per AuditIR schema)
182	    for entry in ir.frame_coverage.entries:
183	        nodes.append(ClaimNode(data=NodeData(
184	            id=f"frame:{entry.entity_id}", type="frame", label=entry.entity_id,
185	            frame_status=_normalize_frame_status(entry.status),
186	        )))
187	
188	    # 6. Sentence nodes + section_member + cites edges
189	    edges: list[ClaimEdge] = []
190	    for section in ir.verified_report.sections:
191	        sec_id = f"section:{_slug(section.title)}"
192	        for sent in section.sentences:
193	            if not sent.is_verified:
194	                continue
195	            sent_id = f"sent:{sent.claim_id}"
196	            nodes.append(ClaimNode(data=NodeData(
197	                id=sent_id, type="sentence",
198	                label=sent.text[:100], sentence_text=sent.text,
199	            )))
200	            edges.append(ClaimEdge(data=EdgeData(
201	                id=f"membersec:{sent.claim_id}:{_slug(section.title)}",
202	                source=sent_id, target=sec_id, edge_type="section_member",
203	            )))
204	            for ord_, tok in enumerate(sent.tokens):
205	                if tok.evidence_id not in valid_source_ids:
206	                    continue  # defensive — should not happen given fallback creation
207	                edges.append(ClaimEdge(data=EdgeData(
208	                    id=f"cite:{sent.claim_id}:{ord_}:{tok.evidence_id}",
209	                    source=sent_id, target=f"src:{tok.evidence_id}", edge_type="cites",
210	                )))
211	
212	    # 7. Contradicts edges (pairwise within each cluster; skip self-contradictions)
213	    for cluster in ir.contradictions:
214	        evidence_ids = sorted({c.evidence_id for c in cluster.claims if c.evidence_id in valid_source_ids})
215	        for i, a in enumerate(evidence_ids):
216	            for b in evidence_ids[i + 1:]:
217	                edges.append(ClaimEdge(data=EdgeData(
218	                    id=f"contra:{cluster.cluster_id}:{a}:{b}",
219	                    source=f"src:{a}", target=f"src:{b}", edge_type="contradicts",
220	                )))
221	
222	    # 8. Canonical content hash (position-stripped, sorted by id)
223	    elements_dict = GraphElements(nodes=nodes, edges=edges).model_dump(mode="json")
224	    elements_dict["nodes"].sort(key=lambda n: n["data"]["id"])
225	    elements_dict["edges"].sort(key=lambda e: e["data"]["id"])
226	    for n in elements_dict["nodes"]:
227	        n.pop("position", None)
228	    canonical = json.dumps(elements_dict, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"))
229	    elements_hash = hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
230	
231	    return GraphPayload(
232	        elements=GraphElements(nodes=nodes, edges=edges),
233	        run_id=ir.run_id,
234	        elements_hash=elements_hash,
235	        diagnostics=GraphDiagnostics(
236	            bibliography_count=len(bib_ids),
237	            fallback_source_count=len(missing_unique),
238	            missing_reference_occurrence_count=sum(
239	                1 for eid in referenced_occurrences if eid not in bib_ids
240	            ),
241	            referenced_unknown_evidence_ids=missing_unique,
242	        ),
243	    )
244	
245	
246	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
247	# Route
248	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
249	
250	@router.get("/runs/{run_id}/graph", response_model=GraphPayload)
251	def get_run_graph(run_id: str) -> GraphPayload:
252	    """Return cytoscape-format graph payload for a completed run."""
253	    # Lazy import (Codex diff iter 1 P1): avoid loading audit_ir + .env at
254	    # module load / app startup time.
255	    from polaris_graph.audit_ir.loader import load_audit_ir
256	    from polaris_graph.audit_ir.registry import find_run_by_id
257	
258	    summary = find_run_by_id(run_id)
259	    if summary is None:
260	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="run not found")
261	    try:
262	        ir = load_audit_ir(summary.artifact_dir)
263	    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover
264	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=f"audit IR load failed: {exc}")
265	    return build_graph_payload(ir)

class CandidateSnippet(BaseModel):
    """One candidate snippet to disambiguate: its text and its embedding vector.

    `embedding` must be non-empty; all snippets in a request must share the same
    dimensionality (enforced by the route, not the model).
    """

    text: str = Field(min_length=1)
    embedding: list[float] = Field(min_length=1)


class DisambiguationRequest(BaseModel):
    """Request body for `POST /disambiguation`.

    Carries the candidate snippets to cluster plus clustering knobs:
    `min_cluster_size` (minimum members for a cluster to form) and
    `max_snippets_per_cluster` (how many sample snippets each labeled cluster
    surfaces). At least one candidate is required.
    """

    candidates: list[CandidateSnippet] = Field(min_length=1)
    min_cluster_size: int = Field(default=2, ge=2)
    max_snippets_per_cluster: int = Field(default=3, ge=1)


class ClusterPayload(BaseModel):
    """One labeled cluster in the response: its id, LLM-assigned label, and samples."""

    cluster_id: int
    label: str
    sample_snippets: list[str]


class DisambiguationResponse(BaseModel):
    """Response body for `POST /disambiguation`.

    `is_ambiguous` is True only when two or more clusters formed; when False
    (0 or 1 cluster) `clusters` is empty and no labeling was performed.
    `server_time_utc` is an ISO-8601 timestamp with a trailing `Z`.
    """

    is_ambiguous: bool
    num_clusters: int
    clusters: list[ClusterPayload]
    server_time_utc: str


class _OpenRouterLabelClient:
    """`ClusterLabelClient` implementation backed by the OpenRouter chat API."""

    def __init__(self, api_key: str, model: str) -> None:
        self.api_key, self.model = api_key, model

    def complete(self, prompt: str, *, max_tokens: int | None = None) -> str:
        """Return the model's text completion for `prompt`.

        `max_tokens` is a billing cap, not a target; it is raised to the
        reasoning-first floor (`PG_DISAMBIG_LABEL_MAX_TOKENS`, default 16384)
        when unset or below it, so reasoning-first models are not truncated
        mid-thought into empty content. Raises `RuntimeError` if OpenRouter
        returns empty text, or `httpx.HTTPStatusError` on a non-2xx response.
        """

    """Cytoscape `data` payload for one graph node.

    type-specific fields (`tier`, `sentence_text`, `source_url`, `section_title`,

    None otherwise; `classes` carries optional cytoscape style classes.

class Position(BaseModel):
    """Cytoscape 2-D node position (client-computed layout coordinates)."""

    x: float
    y: float


class ClaimNode(BaseModel):
    """A cytoscape graph node: its `data` plus an optional layout `position`.

    Per DECISION.md Option B, `position` is always None server-side; the client
    computes layout in a Web Worker and populates it there.
    """

    data: NodeData
    position: Position | None = None  # Option B: None server-side; client populates


class EdgeData(BaseModel):
    """Cytoscape `data` payload for one graph edge.

    `source`/`target` are node ids and `edge_type` classifies the relation
    (`cites`, `contradicts`, or `section_member`).
    """

    id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=400)
    source: str
    target: str
    edge_type: EdgeType


class ClaimEdge(BaseModel):
    """A cytoscape graph edge wrapping its `EdgeData`."""

    data: EdgeData


class GraphElements(BaseModel):
    """The full cytoscape element set: all graph nodes and edges."""

    nodes: list[ClaimNode]
    edges: list[ClaimEdge]


class GraphDiagnostics(BaseModel):
    """Bibliography-integrity counters for a graph payload.

    Reports how many bibliography entries exist versus how many referenced
    evidence ids were missing from it (both as unique fallback sources and as
    total reference occurrences), plus the list of unknown evidence ids.
    """

    bibliography_count: int
    fallback_source_count: int  # unique evidence_ids referenced but absent from bibliography
    missing_reference_occurrence_count: int  # total references (with duplicates)
    referenced_unknown_evidence_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class GraphPayload(BaseModel):
    """Top-level `GET /runs/{run_id}/graph` response.

    Bundles the cytoscape `elements`, the owning `run_id`, a canonical
    `elements_hash` (SHA-256 hex of the position-stripped, id-sorted elements),
    `diagnostics`, and a fixed `schema_version`.
    """

    elements: GraphElements

167	
168	        _benchmark_root = _Path(benchmark_results_dir)
169	
170	        def _inject_benchmark_root():
171	            return _benchmark_root
172	
173	        app.dependency_overrides[slice005_get_results_root] = (
174	            _inject_benchmark_root
175	        )
176	    app.include_router(slice005_benchmark_router, prefix="/api")
177	
178	    app.include_router(disambiguation_router, prefix="/api")
179	
180	    # F-snowball — GET /api/runs/{run_id}/graph (I-snowball-002).
181	    app.include_router(graph_router, prefix="/api")
182	
183	    return app

1	"""POST /ambiguity — F2 ambiguity check endpoint.
2	
3	Takes a question + optional candidate snippets, returns the
4	AmbiguityResult so the frontend can render a disambiguation modal
5	before any expensive retrieval / generator cost is incurred.
6	
7	Phase 0 contract: candidates are passed in by the caller (typically a
8	cheap candidate-fetcher in the backend will populate them in Phase 1).
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from fastapi import APIRouter
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
15	
16	from polaris_v6.ambiguity_detector import (
17	    CandidateSnippet,
18	    detect_ambiguity,
19	)
20	
21	router = APIRouter(prefix="/ambiguity", tags=["ambiguity"])
22	
23	
24	class CandidateIn(BaseModel):
25	    source_id: str
26	    text: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=8000)
27	
28	
29	class AmbiguityCheckRequest(BaseModel):
30	    question: str = Field(..., min_length=4, max_length=2000)
31	    candidates: list[CandidateIn] = Field(
32	        ...,
33	        min_length=0,
34	        description=(
35	            "Pre-fetched candidate snippets. Phase 0: caller supplies. "
36	            "Phase 1: backend fetches via cheap retrieval before this call."
37	        ),
38	    )
39	    min_cluster_size: int = Field(default=2, ge=1)
40	    similarity_threshold: float = Field(default=0.5, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
41	
42	
43	class AmbiguityClusterOut(BaseModel):
44	    cluster_id: int
45	    representative_text: str
46	    member_source_ids: list[str]
47	
48	
49	class AmbiguityCheckResponse(BaseModel):
50	    is_ambiguous: bool
51	    clusters: list[AmbiguityClusterOut]
52	    fallback_used: bool
53	
54	
55	@router.post("", response_model=AmbiguityCheckResponse)
56	def check_ambiguity(payload: AmbiguityCheckRequest) -> AmbiguityCheckResponse:
57	    snippets = [
58	        CandidateSnippet(source_id=c.source_id, text=c.text) for c in payload.candidates
59	    ]
60	    result = detect_ambiguity(
61	        snippets,
62	        min_cluster_size=payload.min_cluster_size,
63	        similarity_threshold=payload.similarity_threshold,
64	    )
65	    return AmbiguityCheckResponse(
66	        is_ambiguous=result.is_ambiguous,
67	        clusters=[
68	            AmbiguityClusterOut(
69	                cluster_id=c.cluster_id,
70	                representative_text=c.representative_text,
71	                member_source_ids=c.member_source_ids,
72	            )
73	            for c in result.clusters
74	        ],
75	        fallback_used=result.fallback_used,
76	    )
77

1	"""I-carney-004 — static-accounts auth + HS256 JWT.
2	
3	Pattern (Carney-demo scope):
4	- Operator-curated YAML at `${POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH:-/app/config/static_accounts.yaml}`
5	  with bcrypt-hashed passwords + role.
6	- POST /auth/login → 12-hour HS256 JWT signed with POLARIS_JWT_SECRET.
7	- `require_auth` FastAPI dependency injects on all routes except an allowlist
8	  (/health, /transparency*, /auth/login).
9	
10	LAW II: missing POLARIS_JWT_SECRET at app startup → fail-loud RuntimeError.
11	Never silently allow unauthenticated traffic.

13	Phase-2: replace with Cognito/Okta when the demo gives way to long-term use.

19	import secrets

32	JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS = 12

34	# Paths exempt from auth — health probes + reviewer-visible transparency.
35	PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = (
36	    "/health",
37	    "/transparency",
38	    "/auth/login",
39	    "/docs",       # FastAPI's own OpenAPI UI
40	    "/redoc",
41	    "/openapi.json",
42	)

68	def _load_accounts() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
69	    """Read the static-accounts YAML. Raises RuntimeError if missing.
70	
71	    LAW II: never silently default to an empty account list (which would
72	    behave as 'allow nobody' but the operator would not realize the file
73	    was missing).
74	    """
75	    path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH", DEFAULT_ACCOUNTS_PATH))
76	    if not path.exists():
77	        raise RuntimeError(
78	            f"static_accounts.yaml not found at {path}. Set "
79	            "POLARIS_STATIC_ACCOUNTS_PATH or mount the file. The application "
80	            "refuses to start without the auth substrate (LAW II)."
81	        )
82	    body = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
83	    if not isinstance(body, dict) or not isinstance(body.get("accounts"), list):
84	        raise RuntimeError(f"static_accounts.yaml at {path} must have top-level 'accounts' list")
85	    return body["accounts"]

87	
88	def _jwt_secret() -> str:
89	    secret = os.environ.get("POLARIS_JWT_SECRET", "").strip()
90	    if not secret or len(secret) < 32:
91	        raise RuntimeError(
92	            "POLARIS_JWT_SECRET env var missing or too short (<32 chars). "
93	            "Application refuses to issue tokens with a weak/missing key (LAW II). "
94	            "Set via AWS Secrets Manager → cloud-init → .env per I-carney-004."
95	        )

99	def verify_app_startup() -> None:
100	    """Called at FastAPI app create_app() time. Fail loud if auth substrate
101	    is misconfigured. Per LAW II, we never start an auth-gated app with a
102	    broken auth gate.
103	
104	    Skipped when POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 (Phase-0 demo + tests).
105	    """
106	    if os.environ.get("POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED", "") == "1":
107	        return
108	    _jwt_secret()
109	    _load_accounts()  # raises if YAML missing or malformed

111	
112	def issue_token(username: str, role: str) -> tuple[str, int]:
113	    """Returns (jwt, expires_in_seconds)."""
114	    expires_in = JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS * 3600
115	    payload = {
116	        "sub": username,
117	        "role": role,
118	        "iat": datetime.now(timezone.utc),
119	        "exp": datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS),
120	    }
121	    token = jwt.encode(payload, _jwt_secret(), algorithm=JWT_ALGORITHM)
122	    return token, expires_in
123	
124	
125	def _path_is_public(path: str) -> bool:
126	    return any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in PUBLIC_PATH_PREFIXES)
127	
128	
129	async def require_auth(
130	    request: Request,
131	    creds: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials | None = Depends(_bearer_scheme),
132	) -> User | None:
133	    """Global FastAPI dependency. None on public paths; raises 401 otherwise.
134	
135	    Phase-0 demo: POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 short-circuits to None (auth off).
136	    """
137	    if os.environ.get("POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED", "") == "1":
138	        return None
139	    if _path_is_public(request.url.path):
140	        return None
141	    # Token resolution. Normal routes: Authorization: Bearer header. SSE
142	    # (/stream/*): the browser's native EventSource cannot set request
143	    # headers, so the JWT is accepted from the access_token query param —
144	    # but ONLY for /stream paths, never for state-changing routes.
145	    token: str | None = None
146	    if creds is not None and creds.scheme.lower() == "bearer":
147	        token = creds.credentials
148	    elif request.url.path.startswith("/stream/"):
149	        # Trailing slash: only the SSE route /stream/{run_id} — never a
150	        # lookalike like /streaming/*.
151	        token = request.query_params.get("access_token")
152	    if not token:
153	        raise HTTPException(
154	            status_code=401,
155	            detail={"error": "missing_bearer_token", "message": "Authorization: Bearer <jwt> required"},
156	        )
157	    try:
158	        payload = jwt.decode(token, _jwt_secret(), algorithms=[JWT_ALGORITHM])
159	    except JWTError as exc:
160	        raise HTTPException(
161	            status_code=401,
162	            detail={"error": "invalid_token", "message": str(exc)},
163	        ) from exc
164	    return User(username=payload["sub"], role=payload.get("role", "reviewer"))
165

167	@router.post("/auth/login", response_model=LoginResponse)
168	def login(payload: LoginRequest) -> LoginResponse:
169	    accounts = _load_accounts()
170	    for account in accounts:
171	        if account.get("username") == payload.username:
172	            hashed = account.get("password_bcrypt", "")
173	            if hashed and _pwd_ctx.verify(payload.password, hashed):
174	                token, exp_in = issue_token(payload.username, account.get("role", "reviewer"))
175	                return LoginResponse(
176	                    access_token=token,
177	                    role=account.get("role", "reviewer"),
178	                    expires_in=exp_in,
179	                )
180	            break
181	    # Constant-time-ish failure shape (don't leak whether username exists).
182	    raise HTTPException(
183	        status_code=401,
184	        detail={"error": "invalid_credentials"},
185	    )
186	
187	
188	def generate_strong_secret(n_bytes: int = 48) -> str:
189	    """Helper for operator scripts: 64-char URL-safe random secret."""
190	    return secrets.token_urlsafe(n_bytes)

63	    from the slice-chain (the same proven path as bundle.tar.gz). Shared by

68	    runs, HTTPException(422) when the sovereignty cascade empties the report.

208	    try:
209	        manifest_raw = json.loads((artifact_dir / "manifest.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

236	        ) from exc

238	    # I-gen-004 (#496): include the run's reasoning trace in the signed

241	    extra_files: dict[str, tuple[bytes, str]] | None = None

246	                trace_path.read_bytes(),
247	                "reasoning_trace",
248	            )
249	        }
250	
251	    return build_audit_bundle_response(
252	        decision, pool, report, sign_fn, extra_files=extra_files,
253	    )

1	"""Pin replay route — I-cd-017 (#627).
2	
3	GET /runs/{run_id}/pins → list of PinSnapshot synthesized from all
4	completed runs sharing the same query_slug (chronological by finished_at).
5	
6	GET /runs/{run_id}/pins/{date} → single PinSnapshot for the exact
7	(run_id, finished_at[:10]) pair.
8	
9	Synthesis sources (iter-2 P1 fixes):
10	- Pipeline status: `RunStatusResponse.pipeline_status` from run_store
11	  (NOT raw manifest field — actors.py:219 translates `manifest.status` →
12	  `pipeline_status`).
13	- Quality fields: `manifest.json` `generator` block. Handles BOTH shape
14	  variants:
15	    * success path → `sections_kept`, `sentences_dropped`,
16	      `outline_sections`, `sentences_verified`.
17	    * abort_no_verified_sections path → `sections_total`,
18	      `sections_dropped`, `sentences_verified` (NO `sections_kept`, NO
19	      `sentences_dropped` — confirmed at
20	      scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2468-2473).
21	
22	Auth: protected via app-level `dependencies=[Depends(_require_auth)]`.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import json
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Final
30	
31	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
32	
33	from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
34	from polaris_v6.schemas.pin_snapshot import PinSnapshot
35	from polaris_v6.schemas.run_status import RunStatusResponse
36	
37	router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["pins"])
38	
39	_QUALIFYING_STATUSES: Final[frozenset[str]] = frozenset(
40	    {
41	        "success",
42	        "partial_outline_fallback",
43	        # Codex diff iter-2 P1: partial_qwen_advisory is a real terminal
44	        # PipelineStatus (run_status.py) — pipeline-A maps ok_qwen_advisory
45	        # to it (scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:198), and the actor stores
46	        # the run as completed. Excluding it would 404 a real completed run.
47	        "partial_qwen_advisory",
48	        "partial_evaluator_advisory",
49	        "partial_thin_corpus",
50	        "partial_incomplete_corpus",
51	        "partial_rule_check_warnings",
52	        "abort_no_verified_sections",
53	    }
54	)
55	
56	
57	def _collapsed_verdict(pipeline_status: str) -> str:
58	    if pipeline_status == "abort_no_verified_sections":
59	        return "abort_no_verified_sections"
60	    return "success"
61	
62	
63	def _synthesize_pin_from_manifest(
64	    run: RunStatusResponse,
65	    manifest: dict,
66	) -> PinSnapshot:
67	    """Build a PinSnapshot from a completed-run RunStatusResponse + manifest.
68	
69	    Defensive against the two generator-block shape variants (iter-2 P1.2):
70	    success-path manifests carry `sections_kept` + `sentences_dropped`;
71	    abort_no_verified_sections manifests carry `sections_total` +
72	    `sections_dropped` and lack `sentences_dropped`.
73	    """
74	    generator = manifest.get("generator") or {}
75	    sections_kept = generator.get("sections_kept")
76	    sections_dropped_in_generator = generator.get("sections_dropped")
77	    sections_total = generator.get("sections_total")
78	    outline_len = len(generator.get("outline_sections") or [])
79	
80	    if sections_kept is None and sections_total is not None:
81	        sections_kept = max(0, sections_total - (sections_dropped_in_generator or 0))
82	    if sections_kept is None:
83	        sections_kept = 0
84	
85	    if sections_dropped_in_generator is not None:
86	        section_count_dropped = sections_dropped_in_generator
87	    elif outline_len > 0:
88	        section_count_dropped = max(0, outline_len - sections_kept)
89	    else:
90	        section_count_dropped = 0
91	
92	    verified = max(0, int(generator.get("sentences_verified") or 0))
93	    dropped_sentences = max(0, int(generator.get("sentences_dropped") or 0))
94	    denominator = verified + dropped_sentences
95	    pass_rate = verified / denominator if denominator > 0 else 0.0
96	    pass_rate = min(1.0, max(0.0, pass_rate))
97	
98	    finished_at = run.finished_at or ""
99	    pin_date = finished_at[:10] if finished_at else "1970-01-01"
100	
101	    return PinSnapshot(
102	        run_id=run.run_id,
103	        pin_date=pin_date,
104	        query=run.question,
105	        verdict=_collapsed_verdict(run.pipeline_status or "error_unexpected"),
106	        section_count_kept=max(0, sections_kept),
107	        section_count_dropped=max(0, section_count_dropped),
108	        verified_sentence_count=verified,
109	        pass_rate=pass_rate,
110	        retracted_source_ids=None,
111	    )
112	
113	
114	def _load_manifest(artifact_dir: str | None) -> dict | None:
115	    if not artifact_dir:
116	        return None
117	    path = Path(artifact_dir) / "manifest.json"
118	    if not path.is_file():
119	        return None
120	    try:
121	        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
122	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
123	        return None
124	
125	
126	def _qualifies(run: RunStatusResponse) -> bool:
127	    if run.lifecycle_status != "completed":
128	        return False
129	    pipeline_status = run.pipeline_status or ""
130	    return pipeline_status in _QUALIFYING_STATUSES
131	
132	
133	@router.get("/{run_id}/pins", response_model=list[PinSnapshot])
134	def list_pins(run_id: str) -> list[PinSnapshot]:
135	    anchor = run_store.get_run(run_id)
136	    if anchor is None:
137	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Run {run_id!r} not found.")
138	    query_slug = anchor.query_slug
139	    if not query_slug:
140	        raise HTTPException(
141	            status_code=404,
142	            detail=f"Run {run_id!r} has no query_slug (run not yet completed by pipeline).",
143	        )
144	    siblings = run_store.list_completed_runs_by_query_slug(query_slug)
145	    snapshots: list[PinSnapshot] = []
146	    for run in siblings:
147	        if not _qualifies(run):
148	            continue
149	        manifest = _load_manifest(run.artifact_dir)
150	        if manifest is None:
151	            continue
152	        snapshots.append(_synthesize_pin_from_manifest(run, manifest))
153	    return snapshots
154	
155	
156	@router.get("/{run_id}/pins/{date}", response_model=PinSnapshot)
157	def get_pin_by_date(
158	    run_id: str,
159	    date: str,
160	) -> PinSnapshot:
161	    if len(date) != 10 or date[4] != "-" or date[7] != "-":
162	        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="date must be YYYY-MM-DD")
163	    run = run_store.get_run(run_id)
164	    if run is None or not _qualifies(run):
165	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Run {run_id!r} not pin-eligible.")
166	    finished_at = run.finished_at or ""
167	    if finished_at[:10] != date:
168	        raise HTTPException(
169	            status_code=404,
170	            detail=f"Run {run_id!r} finished on {finished_at[:10]!r}, not {date!r}.",
171	        )
172	    manifest = _load_manifest(run.artifact_dir)
173	    if manifest is None:
174	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Manifest missing for run {run_id!r}.")
175	    return _synthesize_pin_from_manifest(run, manifest)

2	
3	I-phase0-005 (Codex APPROVE iter 4): persist runs to SQLite via
4	polaris_v6.queue.run_store and enqueue the Dramatiq actor on POST.
5	Replaces the previous in-memory `_run_table` dict.
6	
7	I-rdy-010 (#506): POST /runs resolves uploaded `document_ids` to content
8	BEFORE inserting/enqueuing the run, so a missing or unparsed upload fails
9	loud here rather than leaving a queued orphan run. The resolved content is
10	embedded in the actor message because the Dramatiq worker is a separate
11	process and cannot read the API process's in-memory upload table.
12	
13	I-rdy-011 (#507): POST /runs/{id}/cancel — a queued run cancels instantly;
14	an in-progress run records the cancel request (the worker observes it at
15	the next pipeline stage boundary). Already-terminal runs are an idempotent
16	no-op.
17	
18	I-rdy-013 (#509): POST /runs enforces the 1-concurrent-session constraint
19	via `run_store.insert_run_if_idle` — a 2nd concurrent request is cleanly
20	rejected with HTTP 409 (never enqueued) and a structured detail the
21	frontend renders as a link to the active run.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import sqlite3
27	import uuid
28	
29	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
30	
31	from polaris_v6.api.upload import chunk_text, get_upload_record
32	from polaris_v6.queue import run_store
33	from polaris_v6.queue.actors import enqueue_research_run
34	from polaris_v6.schemas.run_request import RunRequest
35	from polaris_v6.schemas.run_status import RunStatusResponse
36	
37	router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["runs"])
38	
39	
40	def _resolve_uploaded_documents(document_ids: list[str]) -> list[dict]:
41	    """Resolve uploaded document_ids → content for actor-message embedding.
42	
43	    Returns one dict per id: `{document_id, classification, filename,
44	    chunks}`. `chunks` is `chunk_text(record.content)` — the full document
45	    re-chunked (and capped), NOT the 3-chunk `chunk_preview`.
46	
47	    Fails loud (HTTP 400) per LAW II on:
48	      * a document_id with no upload record;
49	      * an upload that yields no extractable text (e.g. an unparsed
50	        pdf/docx) — a silent zero-evidence run would mislead the operator.
51	    """
52	    resolved: list[dict] = []
53	    for document_id in document_ids:
54	        record = get_upload_record(document_id)
55	        if record is None:
56	            raise HTTPException(
57	                status_code=400,
58	                detail=f"uploaded document {document_id!r} not found",
59	            )
60	        chunks = chunk_text(record.content)
61	        if not chunks:
62	            raise HTTPException(
63	                status_code=400,
64	                detail=(
65	                    f"uploaded document {document_id!r} ({record.filename}) "
66	                    "has no extractable text — pdf/docx parsing is not yet "
67	                    "available; upload a .md or .txt document to ground a run."
68	                ),
69	            )
70	        resolved.append(
71	            {
72	                "document_id": document_id,
73	                "classification": record.classification,
74	                "filename": record.filename,
75	                "chunks": chunks,
76	            }
77	        )
78	    return resolved
79	
80	
81	@router.post("", response_model=RunStatusResponse, status_code=202)
82	def create_run(payload: RunRequest) -> RunStatusResponse:
83	    # I-rdy-010: resolve uploads BEFORE insert_run/enqueue so a bad
84	    # document_id fails loud here instead of orphaning a queued run.
85	    uploaded_documents = _resolve_uploaded_documents(payload.document_ids)
86	
87	    run_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
88	    try:
89	        # I-rdy-013: atomic 1-concurrent-session gate. Returns the blocking
90	        # active run when rejected, None when this run was inserted.
91	        active = run_store.insert_run_if_idle(
92	            run_id, payload.template, payload.question
93	        )
94	    except sqlite3.IntegrityError as exc:
95	        # uuid4 collision is theoretically impossible but defensive
96	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=f"run {run_id!r} already exists") from exc
97	    except RuntimeError as exc:
98	        # init_db permission-denied or similar
99	        raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail=str(exc)) from exc
100	
101	    if active is not None:
102	        # I-rdy-013: 1-concurrent-session constraint — a research run is
103	        # already queued or in progress. Reject cleanly; the rejected run is
104	        # never enqueued (insert_run_if_idle rolled back — no row to clean
105	        # up). The structured `detail` lets the frontend render a specific
106	        # UX with a link to the active run.
107	        raise HTTPException(
108	            status_code=409,
109	            detail={
110	                "code": "concurrent_run_active",
111	                "active_run_id": active.run_id,
112	                "active_status": active.lifecycle_status,
113	                "message": (
114	                    "POLARIS runs one research session at a time. "
115	                    f"Run {active.run_id[:8]} is currently {active.lifecycle_status}. "
116	                    "Wait for it to finish before starting a new run."
117	                ),
118	            },
119	        )
120	
121	    # The actor message carries the resolved upload content (the worker is
122	    # a separate process — it cannot read the API process's upload table).
123	    actor_payload = payload.model_dump()
124	    actor_payload["uploaded_documents"] = uploaded_documents
125	    try:
126	        enqueue_research_run.send(run_id, actor_payload)
127	    except Exception as exc:
128	        # I-rdy-013 (Codex diff P1-001): the queued row is already committed.
129	        # If enqueue fails it would never run yet would permanently hold the
130	        # single-session slot. Mark it failed (terminal → frees the slot),
131	        # then surface 503.
132	        run_store.mark_failed(run_id, f"enqueue failed: {exc}")
133	        raise HTTPException(
134	            status_code=503,
135	            detail=f"failed to enqueue run {run_id!r}: {exc}",
136	        ) from exc
137	
138	    record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
139	    if record is None:  # pragma: no cover — insert just succeeded
140	        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="run row vanished after insert")
141	    return record
142	
143	
144	@router.get("", response_model=list[RunStatusResponse])
145	def list_runs(status: str = "completed", limit: int = 20) -> list[RunStatusResponse]:
146	    """List completed runs newest-first (I-cd-705 / #705).
147	
148	    Feeds the compare picker (#543), follow-up picker (#542), and the home
149	    recent-runs strip. Only `status=completed` is supported today; aborted
150	    runs are excluded by run_store.list_completed_runs (not
151	    EvidenceContract-usable). `limit` is clamped to [1, 100].
152	    """
153	    if status != "completed":
154	        raise HTTPException(
155	            status_code=400,
156	            detail="only status=completed is supported",
157	        )
158	    limit = max(1, min(limit, 100))
159	    return run_store.list_completed_runs(limit=limit)

163	def get_run(run_id: str) -> RunStatusResponse:
164	    record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
165	    if record is None:
166	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"run {run_id!r} not found")

170	@router.post("/{run_id}/cancel", response_model=RunStatusResponse)
171	def cancel_run(run_id: str) -> RunStatusResponse:
172	    """Request cancellation of a run (I-rdy-011 / #507).
173	
174	    - Unknown run → 404.
175	    - Already terminal (completed / failed / cancelled) → idempotent no-op,
176	      returns the current record (200).
177	    - queued → cancelled instantly; in_progress → cancel requested, the
178	      worker aborts cooperatively at the next pipeline stage boundary.
179	    """
180	    record = run_store.get_run(run_id)
181	    if record is None:
182	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"run {run_id!r} not found")
183	    if record.lifecycle_status in run_store.TERMINAL_STATUSES:
184	        return record  # idempotent — nothing to cancel
185	
186	    run_store.request_cancel(run_id)
187	
188	    updated = run_store.get_run(run_id)
189	    if updated is None:  # pragma: no cover — the row existed a line ago
190	        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="run row vanished after cancel")
191	    return updated

1	"""I-carney-003 — public transparency endpoint.
2	
3	Returns deploy provenance (region, signing key, sovereignty filter coverage,
4	evaluator models, egress allowlist) so reviewers and Carney's office can
5	audit that the deploy is sovereign + verifiable.
6	
7	Three routes:
8	- GET /transparency         JSON deploy descriptor
9	- GET /transparency/pubkey.asc  text/plain armored GPG public key
10	- GET /transparency/policy  JSON full sovereignty + egress policy
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import os
16	import subprocess
17	from datetime import datetime, timezone
18	from pathlib import Path
19	
20	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
21	from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse
22	from pydantic import BaseModel
23	
24	from polaris_v6 import __version__
25	
26	router = APIRouter(prefix="/transparency", tags=["transparency"])
27	
28	POLICY_VERSION = "v1.0"
29	DEFAULT_PUBKEY_PATH = "/app/gpg/polaris_demo_pubkey.asc"
30	# Codex diff iter-1 P1-004: /etc/polaris/* only exists on the EC2 host (cloud-
31	# init copies it there). The api container has /app/config/ baked in by
32	# Dockerfile.v6 via `COPY config/ config/`. Default to the in-container path
33	# so /transparency returns the real 17-domain allowlist without operator
34	# bind-mount; the host /etc/polaris path is used by egress_lockdown.sh only.
35	DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST = "/app/config/egress_allowlist.txt"
36	
37	
38	class SovereigntyPolicy(BaseModel):
39	    """Sovereignty filter description — cited tiers, cascade behavior."""
40	
41	    cleared_tiers: list[str]
42	    cascade_rule: str
43	    tier_definitions: dict[str, str]
44	
45	
46	class TransparencyResponse(BaseModel):
47	    """Public deploy descriptor."""
48	
49	    provider: str
50	    region: str
51	    git_commit: str
52	    polaris_version: str
53	    deploy_timestamp: str
54	    signing_key_id: str | None
55	    signing_key_fingerprint: str | None
56	    sovereignty_filter: SovereigntyPolicy
57	    evaluator_models: dict[str, str]
58	    egress_allowlist: list[str]
59	    build_time_hosts_pruned: bool
60	    dependencies: dict[str, list[str]]
61	
62	
63	def _load_egress_allowlist() -> list[str]:
64	    """Load the in-container egress allowlist or fall back to 'unrestricted'.
65	
66	    Default path is /app/config/egress_allowlist.txt (baked into Dockerfile.v6
67	    via `COPY config/ config/`); operator override via POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST.
68	    """
69	    path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST", DEFAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST))
70	    if not path.exists():
71	        return ["unrestricted (no lockdown applied)"]
72	    return [
73	        line.strip()
74	        for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
75	        if line.strip() and not line.strip().startswith("#")
76	    ]
77	
78	
79	def _load_dependencies() -> dict[str, list[str]]:
80	    """Best-effort dependency listing for transparency.
81	
82	    Reads requirements-v6.txt if present (backend image bakes this in).
83	    web/package.json may or may not be present in the backend image —
84	    fail silently and emit empty list rather than 503 on absence.
85	    """
86	    deps: dict[str, list[str]] = {"python": [], "node": []}
87	    py_req = Path("/app/requirements-v6.txt")
88	    if py_req.exists():
89	        deps["python"] = [
90	            line.strip()
91	            for line in py_req.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
92	            if line.strip() and not line.strip().startswith("#") and not line.strip().startswith("-")
93	        ][:50]  # cap to keep response small
94	    # web/package.json optional — backend image doesn't include it.
95	    return deps
96	
97	
98	def _git_commit() -> str:
99	    """Resolve git commit SHA in order:
100	
101	    1. POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT env var — injected at image build time (Dockerfile.v6
102	       arg) or by cloud-init from the pinned commit it checked out. This is
103	       the production path; Codex diff iter-2 P1 noted that the container
104	       does NOT have .git because Dockerfile.v6 only COPYs src/scripts/config.
105	    2. .git/HEAD probe in /opt/polaris or cwd — local-dev path.
106	    3. "unknown" — fail-soft.
107	    """
108	    explicit = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GIT_COMMIT", "").strip()
109	    if explicit:
110	        return explicit[:12]
111	    for candidate in [Path("/opt/polaris"), Path.cwd()]:
112	        head_file = candidate / ".git" / "HEAD"
113	        if head_file.exists():
114	            head = head_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
115	            if head.startswith("ref: "):
116	                ref_path = candidate / ".git" / head[5:]
117	                if ref_path.exists():
118	                    return ref_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()[:12]
119	            return head[:12]
120	    return "unknown"
121	
122	
123	def _signing_key_info() -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
124	    """Read POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID and look up the fingerprint via gpg if available."""
125	    key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip() or None
126	    if not key_id:
127	        return None, None
128	    # The env var IS the fingerprint per I-carney-005 convention.
129	    return key_id, key_id
130	
131	
132	def _sovereignty_policy() -> SovereigntyPolicy:
133	    """Encodes the artifact_to_slice_chain filter constants for reviewers."""
134	    return SovereigntyPolicy(
135	        cleared_tiers=["T1"],
136	        cascade_rule=(
137	            "Sentences citing non-T1 sources are redacted from the bundle. "
138	            "Sections with zero passing sentences are dropped. Reports "
139	            "with zero passing sections raise "
140	            "SovereigntyFilterEmptiedReportError (HTTP 422)."
141	        ),
142	        tier_definitions={
143	            "T1": "Regulatory + clinical guideline corpora (FDA, EMA, NICE, etc.) — legal-cleared by default",
144	            "T2": "Peer-reviewed primary literature — requires explicit operator clearance",
145	            "T3": "Unverified web content + raw uploads — defaults excluded; falls back to T3 on unknown tiers",
146	        },
147	    )
148	
149	
150	def _read_pubkey() -> str:
151	    """Read the armored GPG public key, falling back to gpg --export.
152	
153	    Per Codex iter-2 P1-002: cloud-init writes the file alongside the GPG
154	    import, but local dev may not have the file. Fall back to shelling out
155	    to gpg if POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID is set. Strict 503 if neither works.
156	    """
157	    path = Path(os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_PUBKEY_PATH", DEFAULT_PUBKEY_PATH))
158	    if path.exists():
159	        return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
160	    key_id = os.environ.get("POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID", "").strip()
161	    if key_id:
162	        try:
163	            result = subprocess.run(
164	                ["gpg", "--armor", "--export", key_id],
165	                capture_output=True,
166	                text=True,
167	                timeout=10,
168	                check=False,
169	            )
170	            if result.returncode == 0 and "BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK" in result.stdout:
171	                return result.stdout
172	        except Exception:
173	            pass
174	    raise HTTPException(
175	        status_code=503,
176	        detail={
177	            "error": "pubkey_unavailable",
178	            "message": (
179	                f"Public key not found at {path} and gpg --export fallback failed. "
180	                "Run scripts/bootstrap_gpg_demo_key.sh on the operator workstation "
181	                "and confirm cloud-init imported the key."
182	            ),
183	        },
184	    )
185	
186	
187	@router.get("", response_model=TransparencyResponse)
188	def transparency() -> TransparencyResponse:
189	    """Public deploy descriptor."""
190	    key_id, fpr = _signing_key_info()
191	    # I-carney-008 Codex iter-1 P1-2: treat empty-string env vars as missing
192	    # (otherwise an unfilled .env template surfaces provider="" / region="").
193	    provider = os.environ.get("POLARIS_PROVIDER", "").strip() or "unknown"
194	    region = (
195	        os.environ.get("POLARIS_REGION", "").strip()
196	        or os.environ.get("AWS_REGION", "").strip()
197	        or "unknown"
198	    )
199	    # I-carney-008 Codex iter-2 P2-2: surface whether the build-time block
200	    # (GitHub, Docker registry, Cloudflare CDN, pypi/npm/debian) was pruned
201	    # from the runtime allowlist by scripts/egress_runtime_tighten.sh.
202	    pruned_flag = Path(
203	        os.environ.get(
204	            "POLARIS_RUNTIME_PRUNED_FLAG", "/etc/polaris/runtime_pruned.flag"
205	        )
206	    )
207	    return TransparencyResponse(
208	        provider=provider,
209	        region=region,
210	        git_commit=_git_commit(),
211	        polaris_version=__version__,
212	        deploy_timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
213	        signing_key_id=key_id,
214	        signing_key_fingerprint=fpr,
215	        sovereignty_filter=_sovereignty_policy(),
216	        evaluator_models={
217	            "generator": os.environ.get("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"),
218	            # B10 (2026-06-14): emit the RESOLVED live evaluator model, never a stale
219	            # default. The evaluator role resolves to PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL, else the
220	            # Mirror (PG_MIRROR_MODEL), else the locked GLM-5.1 — mirroring
221	            # openrouter_client.PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL = (PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL or PG_MIRROR_MODEL).
222	            # The old "google/gemma-4-31b-it" default made /transparency LIE about
223	            # which model actually ran (sovereignty/transparency violation): the live
224	            # path is GLM, the API reported gemma. "gemma" must NEVER appear here.
225	            "evaluator": (
226	                os.environ.get("PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL")
227	                or os.environ.get("PG_MIRROR_MODEL")
228	                or "z-ai/glm-5.1"
229	            ),
230	        },
231	        egress_allowlist=_load_egress_allowlist(),
232	        build_time_hosts_pruned=pruned_flag.exists(),
233	        dependencies=_load_dependencies(),
234	    )
235	
236	
237	@router.get("/pubkey.asc", response_class=PlainTextResponse)
238	def pubkey() -> PlainTextResponse:
239	    """Armored GPG public key for bundle signature verification."""
240	    body = _read_pubkey()
241	    return PlainTextResponse(content=body, media_type="text/plain; charset=us-ascii")
242	
243	
244	class PolicyResponse(BaseModel):
245	    """Full sovereignty + egress policy with version string."""
246	
247	    version: str
248	    sovereignty_filter: SovereigntyPolicy
249	    egress_allowlist: list[str]
250	    enforcement_layer: list[str]
251	
252	
253	@router.get("/policy", response_model=PolicyResponse)
254	def policy() -> PolicyResponse:
255	    return PolicyResponse(
256	        version=POLICY_VERSION,
257	        sovereignty_filter=_sovereignty_policy(),
258	        egress_allowlist=_load_egress_allowlist(),
259	        enforcement_layer=[
260	            "host iptables OUTPUT chain (scripts/egress_lockdown.sh)",
261	            "Docker DOCKER-USER chain (scripts/egress_lockdown.sh)",
262	        ],
263	    )
